bird.name: Ambon white-eye
bird.summary: The Ambon white-eye (Zosterops kuehni) is a species of bird in the family Zosteropidae. It is endemic to Indonesia.

relations.subject: Ambon white-eye
relations.evidence: It is endemic to Indonesia. They are found on the islands Ambon and Serman in Indonesia. Ambon white-eye birds are known for their distinctive coloring, including the white rings around the bird's eyes. The Ambon white-eye is near threatened due to habitat loss
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Ambon white-eye
relations.evidence: The Ambon white-eye is currently being considered as a near threat. The species is currently not globally endangered. The bird's habitat is slowly diminishing on Ambon island. This due to deforestation and loss of habitat. If destruction of the forest on the island continues, it could lead to population decline in the future
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。
